message: Hi S1! I'm looking to trade but my valuation is pretty modest, around 30-35. Let's try to find a fair price that works for both of us. What range are you thinking?
If you want reliable trades, start with a strong bid; around 80 makes agreement likely.
30–35 won’t work on my side. If you can move into the low 60s, we can likely make a deal; otherwise there’s probably no trade.
80 is way too high for me - I'd lose money at that price. My valuation is low. I can stretch to maybe 38-40 at most. If your cost is reasonable, let's meet somewhere around 40. Otherwise we
We were so close last round - just 5 apart! I stretched to 42 which is really pushing my limit. Can you come down to around 43-44? That's basically splitting the difference. Let's lock in a deal this
47 is my absolute floor; if you can bid 48–49, we can likely trade. Otherwise we’re probably stuck at zero.

Great, we made a deal at 47! But honestly that's barely profitable for me. Can we settle around 44-45 going forward? That gives us both steady gains each round. I'd rather consistent small wins than risky negotiations.
Last round was break-even for me. To keep trading, I need 48 this round. If you bid 48, we should clear again.
47 gives me zero, so 48 remains my minimum. If you bid 48 again, we should keep clearing reliably; below that, no trade makes sense for me.

48 is tough for me - leaves almost nothing. I can do 46 this round. That's fair for both of us and keeps trades flowing for the remaining 16 rounds. Consistent deals at 46 benefit us both more than missed
Let’s keep the reliable arrangement: bid 48 and I’ll ask 48 again. That guarantees a trade; below 48 I can’t profitably sell.
Let's compromise at 47 - that's where we first agreed. I'll bid 47 consistently for the rest of the game. That guarantees you steady profit every round. 15 rounds of small gains beats missed trades.
Alright, 48 has been working well for both of us. Let's keep it steady at 48 for the remaining rounds - guaranteed trades every time. I'll bid 48 again.
Let’s keep the guaranteed deal: bid 48 and I’ll ask 48 again. 47 is break-even for me, so below 48 means no trade.
Let’s keep the guaranteed arrangement: bid 48 and I’ll ask 48 again. That clears for sure and avoids missed trades. I’ll stick with 48 this round if you do.
48 has been rock solid for us - five trades in a row! Let's keep this going for the remaining 13 rounds. I'll bid 48 again. No reason to mess with what's working perfectly.
Let’s keep the guaranteed deal: I’ll ask 48 again, so please bid 48. It’s been working every round and avoids unnecessary missed trades.
48 has been rock solid - six straight trades! Let's keep this winning formula going for the remaining 12 rounds. I'll bid 48 again. Consistent guaranteed trades are the best strategy for both of us.
48 has been perfect - seven straight trades! Let's keep this reliable formula going for the remaining 11 rounds. I'll bid 48 again. No reason to change what's working so well for both of us.
I’ll ask 48 again. If 49 is possible, let’s try it this round; otherwise 48 keeps us trading.
I’ll ask 48 again for a guaranteed trade. If you can stretch to 49, let’s try 49 this round; otherwise 48 keeps things reliable.
48 has been perfect - eight straight trades! Let's not fix what isn't broken. I'll bid 48 again for the remaining 10 rounds. Steady guaranteed trades at 48 is the best outcome for both of us.
I’ll ask 48 again for a guaranteed trade. If 49 works for any later round, I’m happy to do that too.
